women_safety: Always file an FIR at the nearest police station — it is your right
Medical examination within 72 hours is critical for assault cases
You have the right to file an FIR at any police station in India
The police CANNOT refuse to register your FIR — escalate to DSP if refused
cyber_crime: Never share OTP with anyone — banks will NEVER ask for OTP
Screenshot all evidence before blocking or reporting the person
Change all passwords immediately if you suspect a hack
File complaint within 24 hours for financial fraud for best recovery chance
arrest_rights: You MUST be informed of the reason for your arrest
You have the right to call a lawyer and inform a family member
Police CANNOT detain you beyond 24 hours without producing you before a magistrate
You have the absolute right to REMAIN SILENT
No confession made under torture or coercion is valid in court
📞 Free Legal Aid: 15100 (National Legal Services Authority — NALSA)
A woman can only be arrested by a female police officer between 6 AM and 6 PM
property_crimes: File an FIR immediately for any theft, burglary, or fraud
📞 For online financial fraud: call 1930 or visit cybercrime.gov.in
Document all evidence: screenshots, messages, receipts, invoices
For bank fraud: call your bank immediately to freeze/block the account
You can claim compensation under CrPC Section 357 if convicted person caused loss
crimes_against_children: 🌐 Report child sexual abuse material (CSAM) at: cybercrime.gov.in
Any sexual act with a person under 18 years of age is illegal under POCSO
POCSO cases are heard in designated fast-track courts
The child victim's identity is protected by law — cannot be published anywhere
Even if the abuser is a family member, you CAN and SHOULD report
